# UC-19.1.48 · Dell VxRail vSAN Unhealthy vSAN Objects and Resync Backlog

## Description

On VxRail, vSAN object health translates directly to VM availability. Inaccessible components and large resync backlogs signal imminent data risk or recent host/disk events that need validation before the next failure.

## Value

Prioritizes HCI incidents that threaten VM I/O by combining vSAN health state with resync depth in one operator view.

## Implementation

Schedule a vSAN health capture job (PowerCLI or VMware Aria Operations export to HEC) and key it by `cluster_id` shared with other VxRail sourcetypes. Tune resync threshold to your cluster size. Correlate with `vxrail:physical_disk` and host maintenance windows.

## SPL

```spl
index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vsan" earliest=-2h
| eval unhealthy=if(vsan_health!="healthy" OR inaccessible_objects>0 OR resync_bytes_remaining>107374182400, 1, 0)
| where unhealthy=1
| stats latest(vsan_health) as health, latest(inaccessible_objects) as inacc, latest(resync_bytes_remaining) as resync_left by cluster_id
| eval resync_gb=round(resync_left/1073741824,2)
| table cluster_id, health, inacc, resync_gb
| sort -inacc
```

## Visualization

Single value (clusters unhealthy), Bar chart (resync GB by cluster), Table (inaccessible object counts).

## References

- [VMware Tech Zone — vSAN operations and health](https://core.vmware.com/vmware-vsan)
